title: Rakatan ruins (Dantooine)
content:

{{Quote|The dark side is strong in this place – I can feel its power!|[[Darth Malak|Malak]]|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
The '''Rakatan ruins''' were all that remained of an ancient structure built on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] by the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] during the height of their [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]]. The temple itself was built as a monument to the power of the [[Star Forge]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] in the [[Lehon system]] which could be located using the information stored in a [[Star Map]] that was housed inside the temple.<ref name="KotOR" />

After the fall of the Infinite Empire, the building was abandoned and forgotten for thousands of years. Before the outbreak of the [[Jedi Civil War]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak]] learned of the existence of the Star Forge and set out to find the Rakatan [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. They discovered the ruins and accessed the star map located inside. Although the data had been corrupted, the two were able to retrieve the coordinates of other planets that were once ruled by the Rakata. By investigating each world, they hoped to find more relics of the Infinite Empire that would provide clues to the Star Forge's location. Eventually, they succeeded.<ref name="KotOR" />

[[File:Dantooine-ruins-vision.jpg|thumb|left|275px|The [[Star Map]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] opens for [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak]].]]

Revan and Malak's fall to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] was completed when they [[Mission to Dromund Kaas|became the pawns]] of the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] of the resurgent [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]], who ordered them to seek out the Star Forge. However, as they approached the Star Forge,<ref name="T8">{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War|int=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War}}</ref> the two new [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords]] broke free of the Emperor's mental control, believing his instructions to be their own desires.<ref name="Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref> They would make use of the Star Forge to create [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] of [[Warship/Legends|warships]] with which they would attempt to conquer the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. During the subsequent [[Jedi Civil War]], Darth Revan was captured by a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] strike team led by [[Bastila Shan]]. His mind was badly damaged during the event and the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to erase all memories of who he was and replaced them with an identity loyal to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]].<ref name="KotOR" />

[[File:Starmap2.jpg|thumb|175px|A Star Map in the ruins on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].]]

Following his [[rescue of Bastila Shan]] on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], Revan was given once again [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]], and progressed to the rank of [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] at the [[Jedi Enclave]] on Dantooine. Meanwhile, the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|Jedi Council]] had appointed the task of investigating the ruins to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Nemo]], whose failure was later assessed by Revan and Bastila. Once again inside the ruins, Revan found Nemo's body, and an ancient [[Overseer (droid)|droid Overseer]] who guarded the entrance to the Star Map. After cycling through several alien dialects in an attempt to communicate, Revan recognized what seemed to be an archaic dialect of the [[Selkath (language)|Selkath]] spoken on [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]]. The Overseer told Revan that he had to be tested in the proving grounds, in order to prove himself worthy of accessing knowledge of the Star Forge, yet, the Overseer did not provide much information on the topic related to [[Rakata/Legends|the Builders]]. It was apparent that the Jedi Nemo had failed the assigned tasks and was killed as a result by the ancient defense system of the temple.<ref name="KotOR" />

Revan proved himself worthy and activated the Star Map, thus beginning a journey to retrace his own steps towards the Star Forge and there confront his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] Darth Malak. An amateur [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologist]] by the name of [[Casus Sandral]] also made an effort to discover the source of the ancient ruins, and found some correlation between them and something called the [[Rakata/Legends|"Rak"]].<ref name="KotOR" />

[[File:Azkulsmercenaries-encampment.png|thumb|right|300px|[[Azkul's mercenaries]] in the ruins on the surface.]]

Near the close of the Jedi Civil War, a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] battle group under the command of Malak attacked the nearby Jedi Enclave and destroyed it. During the fighting, the Rakatan Ruins were also bombarded and obliterated, likely in an attempt to prevent others from discovering the Star Forge's location. After the bombardment, a group of [[Azkul's mercenaries|mercenaries]], led by [[Azkul]], installed a [[Mercenary enclave|camp]] near the ruins, though no one possessed knowledge of their significance.<ref name="TSL" />
